questions.question: What is the primary semiconductor material used for fabricating the vast majority of modern integrated circuits?
In a set-associative cache, when a cache set is full and a new block needs to be brought in, which cache replacement policy evicts the block that has not been used for the longest period of time?

In digital circuit timing analysis, what is the term for the minimum delay through any path from an input to an output, often critical for verifying that a circuit does not produce an incorrect output too quickly (i.e., for hold time checks)?

In the digital IC design verification flow, which formal verification technique is primarily used to mathematically prove that two different representations of a design (e.g., RTL and gate-level netlist) implement the exact same functionality?

In modern many-core processors and System-on-Chip (SoC) designs, what phenomenon refers to the increasing inability to simultaneously power on and utilize all available transistors on a chip due to power density and thermal constraints, leading to a significant portion of the silicon remaining unpowered or "dark" at any given time?
